rules.title: Rules of Medieval Chess
rules.intro: Medieval chess differs from modern chess in several key ways. The queen and bishop move very differently, and pawn movement changes as the game progresses.

queen.title: The Queen (Alferza) — Grace Jump
queen.description: At the start of the game, queens are 'Grace Jump' pieces. On their very first move, they have special jumping abilities. After moving once, they become regular queens with limited movement.
queen.firstMoveCaption: Jumps 2 squares (no capture), or 1 diagonal (capture OK)

bishop.title: The Bishop (Elephant)
bishop.description: Unlike modern chess where bishops slide along diagonals, the medieval bishop jumps exactly 2 squares diagonally. It leaps over any piece in between — nothing can block its path.
bishop.movementCaption: Jumps over pieces — the pawn doesn't block the top-left move

rook.title: The Rook
rook.description: The rook moves exactly as in modern chess — it slides any number of squares along a rank or file, and cannot jump over pieces.
rook.movementLabel: Rook movement — slides along ranks and files:
rook.movementCaption: Slides until blocked — can capture the pawn

knight.title: The Knight
knight.description: The knight moves exactly as in modern chess — in an L-shape (2+1 squares), and can jump over other pieces.

pawn.title: The Pawn
pawn.description: Pawns move forward one square, or capture one square diagonally — just like modern chess. However, the double-move on the first turn is only available if no capture has occurred in the game yet.
pawn.note: Once any piece captures another (by either side), all pawns lose the ability to move two squares. Pawns promote to a Grace Jump Queen.
pawn.beforeCaptureLabel: Before any capture — can move 1 or 2 squares:
pawn.afterCaptureLabel: After a capture happened — only 1 square:

king.title: The King
king.description: The king moves exactly as in modern chess — one square in any direction. Castling is available under the standard conditions.
king.movementLabel: King movement — 1 square in any direction:

summary.title: Key Differences from Modern Chess
summary.queen: Starts with special Grace Jump powers (2-square jumps). After first move, only moves 1 square diagonally. Much weaker than the modern queen.
summary.bishop: Jumps exactly 2 squares diagonally (like a knight but diagonal). Can leap over pieces. Does not slide.
summary.pawns: Double-move is only available before any capture occurs in the game. Promote to Grace Jump Queen.
summary.others: Move exactly as in modern chess.

play.title: Medieval Chess
play.freePlayStory: Set up positions and explore medieval chess variants. The queen starts as a Grace Jump piece — it can jump 2 squares diagonally or orthogonally, or capture 1 square diagonally. After its first move, it becomes a regular queen (1 square diagonal only). Bishops jump 2 squares diagonally. Pawns can only advance 2 squares if no capture has occurred.
play.noMoves: No moves yet. Click a piece to start.

create.title: Create Puzzle
create.signInRequired: Please sign in to create puzzles.
create.step1Description: Enter a FEN string to set the puzzle's starting position.
create.step2Description: Make the moves that form the puzzle's solution on the board. Include both the player's moves and the opponent's responses.
create.placeholderDescription: e.g., White to play and checkmate in 2
